response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 20 years of experience, they are a poor fit for this capital markets role. The resume shows minimal C++ expertise (6 months at National Instruments; all recent roles are web/backend with TypeScript/Node.js), no Windows platform development, and critically, zero financial services or capital markets domain knowledge. The mandatory requirements for 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows and either fixed-income expertise or investment banking domain experience are not met.
strengths: Extensive software engineering background (20 years) with demonstrated mastery of multiple domains and languages
Proven communication and mentoring abilities through workshops, talks, and community contributions
Strong CI/CD, infrastructure, and DevOps experience with Docker, GitLab, and deployment automation
concerns: Critical gap: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience versus mandatory 5–10 years; entire recent career is web/backend-focused (Node.js, TypeScript, React)
No evidence of Windows platform development; no financial services, capital markets, fixed-income, or investment banking domain knowledge
SQL experience is limited to MySQL and NoSQL (MongoDB); no demonstrated expertise with SQL Server or Oracle as required

key_factors.factor: Strong general software engineering and communication skills
